headline: These Stocks Are Today’s Movers: Palantir, Intel, AMD, ON Semi, Pinterest, Duolingo, and More
summary: Stock futures were rising Tuesday as investors shrugged off a flare-up of tensions in the Middle East.  Palantir Technologies  slid 3.3% even as the data-wrangling software developer’s first-quarter earnings topped Wall Street’s expectations on all key metrics.  Because it trades at such a lofty valuation, with the stock fetching 97 times expected earnings per share for the next 12 months, Palantir gets judged not on whether it beats expectations but how much it beats estimates.

headline: PayPal Earnings Beat Estimates, but the Rest of the Release Left More Questions for the Stock.
summary: PayPal  stock barely moved Tuesday after the company reported better-than-expected earnings but issued a downbeat outlook for the current quarter.  The payments giant reported adjusted earnings of $1.34 a share for the first quarter, up from $1.33 a year ago and ahead of analysts’ consensus estimate of $1.27, per FactSet.  PayPal reiterated its full-year guidance for a “low-single digit decline to slightly positive” growth in adjusted earnings per share.

PayPal reiterated its full-year guidance for a “low-single digit decline to slightly positive” growth in adjusted earnings per share.

headline: Dow Dips 550 Points Amid Iran War Flareup: Investor Sentiment Declines, But Fear Index Remains In 'Greed' Zone
summary: CNN Money Fear and Greed index fell, but remained in Greed zone. Stocks down on Iran tensions, factory orders up, sectors mostly negative.

headline: Pfizer, Pinterest And 3 Stocks To Watch Heading Into Tuesday
